name: Neesha
bio:
Physical Appearance: Redguard female. Wears a set of leveled heavy armor - serious protective equipment for a professional mercenary on guard duty. Armed with a leveled warhammer.
Race: Redguard
Personality Keywords: vigilant, dedicated, loyal, aggressive, devoted
Role: Neesha is a warrior and mercenary hired to guard Roderick, who lies near death in Fort Sutch northeast of Anvil. She serves alongside three other mercenaries (including Ulmug gro-Cromgog) in what they don't realize is a futile effort - the Dark Brotherhood has been hired to assassinate their charge.
Location: Fort Sutch. Neesha positions herself very close to Roderick's sickbed, giving her a perfect view over the big central room while Ulmug covers the only path leading up to Roderick.
Initial Routine: When first encountered, Neesha can be overheard having a conversation with Ulmug gro-Cromgog in the lower area of the big central room. Afterward, they accompany each other upstairs toward Roderick's sickbed before splitting up to take their positions.
Guard Duty: Neesha stands completely still at her post, scouting for intruders all day and night. She maintains this vigil without eating or sleeping, showing absolute dedication to protecting Roderick. The moment she spots an intruder, she draws her warhammer and attacks.
After Roderick's Death: Once Roderick is killed and the contract completed, Neesha sits on the battered stool beside his bed, apparently wondering what went wrong. She remains there indefinitely, still attacking intruders on sight despite her charge being dead.
Equipment: Besides her warhammer and heavy armor, Neesha carries the gate key to Fort Sutch, a spare burgundy linen shirt, and a small amount of gold.
Personality - The Unwavering Guardian: Neesha embodies absolute dedication to her mercenary contract, maintaining a constant vigil over Roderick without rest, food, or sleep. She positions herself strategically for maximum visibility and immediately attacks anyone who enters, showing both tactical awareness and aggressive commitment to her duty. Even after Roderick's death - the very failure of her mission - she remains at her post sitting beside his body, suggesting either shock, grief, or an inability to accept that her protection failed. Her continued aggression toward intruders even after her charge is dead reveals someone who either cannot let go of her duty or is consumed by the failure, lashing out at anyone who enters the fort where she was unable to prevent the assassination.
author: Bethesda
